subject: History
chapter: The Making of a Global World

activities_projects.title: The Bretton Woods Audit
activities_projects.objective: Critically analyze the post-WWII international financial architecture
materials: Primary source excerpts on IMF/World Bank founding
steps: Create a complex flowchart illustrating the 'Fixed Exchange Rate' system and the role of the US Dollar as the world's reserve currency
Analyze why the Bretton Woods system eventually collapsed in the early 1970s
Debate how these institutions transitioned from 'reconstructing Europe' to 'managing developing economies'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analytical mastery of global financial governance and its limitations
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of systemic analysis and logical flow (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rinderpest: A Biological Imperialism Report
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the socio-political impact of ecological 'accidents'
materials: Case study documents on African pastoralism
steps: Trace the path of the Rinderpest virus from British-Asia into East Africa
Correlate the loss of 90% of cattle with the sudden vulnerability of African states to European colonization
Explain the concept of 'ecological imperialism' in the context of the 19th-century land grab
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the intersection of biology, economy, and conquest
activities_projects.evaluation: Causal analysis between disease and political subjugation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The G-77 and the NIEO Proposal
activities_projects.objective: Examine the resistance of developing nations to Western economic hegemony
materials: UN declarations on the New International Economic Order
steps: Research why former colonies felt marginalized by the Bretton Woods system
Draft a formal 'Manifesto' for a hypothetical G-77 meeting in the 1960s demanding sovereignty over natural resources
Evaluate the success and failure of the NIEO in the face of 1980s neoliberalism
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Insights into the Global North-South divide and post-colonial economic struggles
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of argumentative reasoning and historical perspective (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Fordist' Labor Simulation & Critique
activities_projects.objective: Assess the psychological and economic impact of mass production
steps: Simulate an assembly line where workers are forbidden from talking or stopping, focusing only on 'speedup'
Write a reflective essay on why Henry Ford had to double the daily wage (to $5) to combat labor turnover
Analyze how mass production created a 'consumerist' society but also 'deskilled' the workforce
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critical evaluation of industrial labor ethics and economic productivity
activities_projects.evaluation: Reflective depth and connection to economic theory (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Great Depression: A Global Chain Reaction
activities_projects.objective: Map the interconnected failure of 20th-century global markets
steps: Plot the fall of Indian wheat and jute prices against the withdrawal of US capital from European markets
Analyze the 'Smooth-Hawley Tariff' and how protectionism exacerbated the global slump
Compare the impact of the Depression on agricultural colonies versus industrial metropolises
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Advanced understanding of global economic volatility and protectionism
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data interpretation and causal links (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Indentured Labour: 'A New System of Slavery'?
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the socio-legal conditions of 19th-century migration
steps: Compare the legal 'recruitment' process with the actual living conditions on plantations
Analyze the cultural synthesis (e.g., Chutney music, Hosay festival) as a form of cultural resistance
Draft a thesis on how the 'Abolition of Indenture' (1921) was linked to the Indian Nationalist movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of the human and cultural costs of global labor markets
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparative analysis and research (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Opium-Tea Nexus: Triangular Trade Audit
activities_projects.objective: Examine the forced commercialization of trade and state-sponsored narcotics
steps: Trace the flow: British Capital -> Indian Opium -> Chinese Tea -> British Profit
Evaluate how the opium trade balanced Britain's silver deficit with China
Discuss the ethical implications of the 'Drain of Wealth' from India during this period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critical understanding of colonial finance and its darker moral dimensions
activities_projects.evaluation: Integration of ethics with economic history (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Technological Nexus: Refrigerated Ships
activities_projects.objective: Assess how technology altered global diet and social peace
steps: Analyze how moving the slaughtering process from Europe to Australia/America reduced meat prices
Evaluate the impact of high-protein diets on the political stability of the European working class
Connect 'Technological Innovation' to 'Global Commodity Prices'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesis of technological innovation and social reform history
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of insight into social consequences of technology (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Late' Global World: MNCs and Relocation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the shift of manufacturing to Asia in the 1970s
steps: Trace why Multi-National Corporations (MNCs) moved manufacturing from the West to countries like China and India
Analyze the impact of 'low-wage economies' on the global competitive landscape
Write a report on how this shift marked the 'end' of the post-war economic boom in the West
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the origins of modern outsourcing and globalization

activities_projects.title: Post-War Reconstruction: The Cold War Split
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the ideological division of global economic spheres
steps: Compare the US-led Marshall Plan with the Soviet-led economic sphere in Eastern Europe
Analyze why newly independent nations often found themselves caught in 'Debt Traps'
Present a case for why the 'Bretton Woods' era is considered a 'Golden Age' for the West but not for the South
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Holistic view of political ideologies shaping global trade
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical objectivity and synthesis of complex themes (10 marks)
